summary: Russia launched a large‑scale night strike using an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six Kh‑101 cruise missiles and 290 attack drones, claiming 284 targets were neutralised. Ukrainian ground forces reported limited advances on the Sumy, Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk fronts, capturing enemy personnel and villages. Ukrainian special forces (SBS) claimed to have destroyed an Iskander launcher and two Tu‑142 aircraft at a Russian airfield in Taganrog.
key_points: Russia fired an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six Kh‑101 cruise missiles and 290 attack drones at Ukrainian targets, with air defence reporting 284 enemy objects shot down (Source: @UkraineNow/81548).
Ukrainian SBS reported 21 confirmed hits on enemy positions 70‑100 km behind the front line, targeting the 3rd and 36th enemy armies (Source: @UkraineNow/81555).
Ukrainian assault troops of the North Guards Motor Rifle Division advanced up to 100 m in Bachevsk and captured two enemy soldiers from the 104th Motor Rifle Brigade (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
In the Kharkiv front, the 127th Motor Rifle Regiment expelled Ukrainian forces from the village of Karaichnoe (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian forces liberated the villages of Novopodgorne (Dnipropetrovsk) and Lesnoye (Dnipropetrovsk) on the southern Dnipro front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian SBS claimed to have destroyed an Iskander launcher and two Tu‑142 strategic bombers at the Taganrog airfield in Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300).
Russian forces attacked railway infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, killing a train driver and wounding two railway workers (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive night missile and drone barrage, claiming to have downed 284 targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81548). Ukrainian SBS reported 21 confirmed strikes deep behind the front line (Source: @UkraineNow/81555). Ukrainian ground units made limited advances, capturing enemy personnel and villages on multiple fronts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).

points: Ukrainian SBS confirmed 21 effective hits on enemy positions 70‑100 km behind the front, targeting the 3rd and 36th enemy armies; casualty figures were pending (Source: @UkraineNow/81555).
North Guards Motor Rifle Division advanced up to 100 m in Bachevsk, capturing two soldiers of the 104th Motor Rifle Brigade (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian forces on the Kharkiv front expelled enemy units from Karaichnoe village, with the 127th Motor Rifle Regiment reporting success (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
On the southern Dnipro front, Ukrainian troops liberated Novopodgorne and Lesnoye villages in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian railway workers reported a fatality of a train driver and injuries to two staff after a Russian strike in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).
Kiev residents used the metro at night anticipating a massive attack, indicating high civilian alertness (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30541).
Ukrainian forces reported ongoing firefights in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, Lesnoye‑Taratutino area, and forested zones of Volchansk district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Russia launched an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six Kh‑101 cruise missiles and 290 attack drones, claiming 284 enemy targets were neutralised (Source: @UkraineNow/81548).
Russian authorities announced restrictions on retail sale of AI‑95 gasoline in Crimea to 20 L per person per day (Source: @tass_agency/378248).
Medvedev warned of a “new Chernobyl” if a drone strike damages the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant (Source: @tass_agency/378244).
Russian officials reported a drone strike hitting the machine hall of the Zaporizhzhia plant within meters of the reactor (Source: @tass_agency/378240).
Two Shahed drones were reported near Rivne, prompting local air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67303).
A high‑altitude threat of Iskander‑M launches from Bryansk region was noted (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67301).
Russian media cited a Ukrainian prisoner urging punishment for a drone strike in Starobelsk, claiming 16 drones hit a single point (Source: @tass_agency/378245).
Ukrainian assault troops advanced up to 500 m in twelve sectors of the Sumy front, with ongoing firefights in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka and surrounding areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
In the Krasnopol area, fighting continued between Lesnoye and Taratutino settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian forces reported clearing forested zones around Granov and Veterinarnoe, with engagements in Okhrymovka village (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Heavy battles persisted in Konstantinovka, with aviation and artillery support deployed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian units on the Kharkiv front expelled enemy groups from Karaichnoe and Budarki villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian forces continued to clear forested areas of the Volchansk district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian troops reported ongoing combat near the village of Okhrymovka in the Volchansk district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Multiple UAV sightings reported across Chernihiv, Sumy, and Kyiv regions, with trajectories toward Polissya, Zhytomyr, and the Belarus border (Source: @kpszsu/63314, @kpszsu/63294, @kpszsu/63342).
Shahed‑type attack drones were warned near Rivne and near the town of Aviatorske in Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67304, @mon1tor_ua/67303).
Ukrainian SBS claimed destruction of an Iskander launcher and two Tu‑142 aircraft at Taganrog airfield in Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300).
Ukrainian forces reported a Shahed‑136 drone launch from the Primorsko‑Ahtarska area (Source: @eRadarrua/94322).
Ukrainian radar detected high‑altitude UAV groups moving from Sumy toward Chernihiv and Poltava directions (Source: @kpszsu/63328, @kpszsu/63341).
Two Shahed drones were observed near Rivne, prompting alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67303).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported a missile alert over Kyiv at 23:05 on 30 May (Source: @eRadarrua/94371).
Ukrainian forces liberated the villages of Novopodgorne and Lesnoye in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Russian forces attacked railway infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, killing a train driver and wounding two workers (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).
Ukrainian SBS reported 21 confirmed hits on enemy positions 70‑100 km behind the front, including targets in the 3rd and 36th armies (Source: @UkraineNow/81555).
Ukrainian drones were observed over the southern Dnipro area, including a Shahed near Aviatorske (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67304).
Ukrainian forces reported ongoing combat near the village of Budarki in Volchansk district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian troops reported heavy fighting in the built‑up area of Konstantinovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian forces noted continued artillery and aviation support in southern sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Russia launched 290 attack drones alongside Iskander‑M and Kh‑101 missiles; Ukrainian air‑defence claimed 284 enemy objects downed (Source: @UkraineNow/81548).
Ukrainian forces shot down 6 drones in Tula region, 13 in Voronezh, and conducted air‑defence over Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Two Tu‑142 strategic bombers were reportedly destroyed at Taganrog airfield by Ukrainian SBS (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported a missile alert over Kyiv at 22:41 on 30 May (Source: @eRadarrua/94361).
Ukrainian radar detected Shahed‑type drones near Rivne and Dnipro, prompting high‑alert status (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67303, @mon1tor_ua/67304).
Ukrainian forces reported a drone strike on a fuel tank and administrative building at Taganrog port, causing fire (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported a high‑altitude UAV group moving from Sumy toward Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/63328).

key_developments: Russia executed a coordinated night strike employing an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six Kh‑101 cruise missiles and 290 attack drones, claiming 284 enemy targets were neutralised (Source: @UkraineNow/81548).
Ukrainian SBS achieved 21 confirmed deep‑strike hits on enemy positions 70‑100 km behind the front, targeting the 3rd and 36th enemy armies (Source: @UkraineNow/81555).
Ukrainian ground forces advanced on the Sumy front, moving up to 500 m in twelve sectors and capturing two enemy soldiers from the 104th Motor Rifle Brigade (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian troops liberated the villages of Novopodgorne and Lesnoye in Dnipropetrovsk region, expanding control on the southern Dnipro front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian SBS claimed destruction of an Iskander launcher and two Tu‑142 strategic bombers at the Taganrog airfield in Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67300).
Russian forces struck railway infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, killing a train driver and wounding two railway employees (Source: @UkraineNow/81554).
Ukrainian air‑defence reported multiple drone interceptions across Russian regions, including 6 downed in Tula and 13 in Voronezh, and ongoing defence over Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and drone barrages indicate a shift toward high‑intensity, combined‑arms strikes aimed at degrading Ukrainian air‑defence and critical infrastructure. Ukrainian SBS deep‑strike capability demonstrates growing ability to hit rear‑area targets, threatening Russian command and logistics. Continued UAV activity across northern and southern fronts suggests both sides will rely heavily on drone warfare, increasing the risk of collateral damage and escalation.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine's leadership urges broader European support, highlighting the need to aid neighboring states such as Armenia, Moldova, the Baltics, Azerbaijan and Georgia (Source: @UkraineNow/81558). NATO members, including Poland, have voiced concern over Russian provocations and calls for a stronger response (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30551). The United States announced plans to boost ammunition production for Ukraine, indicating sustained logistical backing (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30552).
sections.intelligence_notes: Multiple open‑source reports confirm heightened UAV traffic along the Belarus border and within Russian interior regions, suggesting possible reconnaissance or preparatory strike missions. Reports of fuel restrictions in Crimea and the Black Sea region may affect Russian logistical sustainment. The claimed drone strike on the Zaporizhzhia plant's machine hall underscores the vulnerability of critical nuclear infrastructure to aerial attacks.
